sources: DoD NIST SP 800-171 Assessment Methodology v1.2.1 (SPRS point values)
verified: 2026-07-03: 110 requirements, 320 assessment objectives; SPRS weight distribution (44x5, 14x3, 51x1, SSP special) reproduces the -203 minimum; AO text spot-checked against Assessment Guide Level 2 v2.13

requirements.name: Authorized Access Control
requirements.statement: Limit system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices (including other systems).

assessment_objectives.a: authorized users are identified
assessment_objectives.b: processes acting on behalf of authorized users are identified
assessment_objectives.c: devices (and other systems) authorized to connect to the system are identified
assessment_objectives.d: system access is limited to authorized users
assessment_objectives.e: system access is limited to processes acting on behalf of authorized users
assessment_objectives.f: system access is limited to authorized devices (including other systems)

examine: System configuration settings and associated documentation
List of active system accounts and the name of the individual associated with each account
Notifications or records of recently transferred, separated, or terminated employees
List of conditions for group and role membership
List of recently disabled system accounts along with the name of the individual associated with each account
List of devices and systems authorized to connect to organizational systems
test: Organizational processes for managing system accounts
Mechanisms for implementing account management

requirements.name: Transaction & Function Control
requirements.statement: Limit system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute.

assessment_objectives.a: the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute are defined
assessment_objectives.b: system access is limited to the defined types of transactions and functions for authorized users

examine: List of approved authorizations including remote access authorizations
System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Mechanisms implementing access control policy

requirements.name: Control CUI Flow
requirements.statement: Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations.

assessment_objectives.a: information flow control policies are defined
assessment_objectives.b: methods and enforcement mechanisms for controlling the flow of CUI are defined
assessment_objectives.c: designated sources and destinations (e.g., networks, individuals, and devices) for CUI within the system and between interconnected systems are identified
assessment_objectives.d: authorizations for controlling the flow of CUI are defined
assessment_objectives.e: approved authorizations for controlling the flow of CUI are enforced

examine: System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Mechanisms implementing information flow enforcement policy

requirements.name: Separation of Duties
requirements.statement: Separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion.

assessment_objectives.a: the duties of individuals requiring separation are defined
assessment_objectives.b: responsibilities for duties that require separation are assigned to separate individuals
assessment_objectives.c: access privileges that enable individuals to exercise the duties that require separation are granted to separate individuals

examine: Procedures addressing divisions of responsibility and separation of duties
System configuration settings and associated documentation
List of divisions of responsibility and separation of duties
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for defining divisions of responsibility and separation of duties
test: Mechanisms implementing separation of duties policy

requirements.name: Least Privilege
requirements.statement: Employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts.

assessment_objectives.a: privileged accounts are identified
assessment_objectives.b: access to privileged accounts is authorized in accordance with the principle of least privilege
assessment_objectives.c: security functions are identified
assessment_objectives.d: access to security functions is authorized in accordance with the principle of least privilege

examine: System configuration settings and associated documentation
List of active system accounts and the name of the individual associated with each account
List of conditions for group and role membership
Notifications or records of recently transferred, separated, or terminated employees
List of recently disabled system accounts along with the name of the individual associated with each account
List of security functions (deployed in hardware, software, and firmware) and security-relevant information for which access is to be explicitly authorized
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for defining least privileges necessary to accomplish specified tasks
test: Organizational processes for managing system accounts
Mechanisms for implementing account management
Mechanisms implementing least privilege functions
Mechanisms prohibiting privileged access to the system

requirements.name: Non-Privileged Account Use
requirements.statement: Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing nonsecurity functions.

assessment_objectives.a: nonsecurity functions are identified
assessment_objectives.b: users are required to use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing nonsecurity functions

examine: List of system-generated security functions assigned to system accounts or roles
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for defining least privileges necessary to accomplish specified organizational tasks
test: Mechanisms implementing least privilege functions

requirements.name: Privileged Functions
requirements.statement: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs.

assessment_objectives.a: privileged functions are defined
assessment_objectives.b: non-privileged users are defined
assessment_objectives.c: non-privileged users are prevented from executing privileged functions
assessment_objectives.d: the execution of privileged functions is captured in audit logs

examine: Privacy and security policies, procedures addressing system use notification
Documented approval of system use notification messages or banners
User acknowledgements of notification message or banner
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for defining least privileges necessary to accomplish specified tasks
test: Mechanisms implementing least privilege functions for non-privileged users
Mechanisms auditing the execution of privileged functions

requirements.name: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
requirements.statement: Limit unsuccessful logon attempts.

assessment_objectives.a: the means of limiting unsuccessful logon attempts is defined
assessment_objectives.b: the defined means of limiting unsuccessful logon attempts is implemented

examine: System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Mechanisms implementing access control policy for unsuccessful logon attempts

requirements.name: Privacy & Security Notices
requirements.statement: Provide privacy and security notices consistent with applicable CUI rules.

assessment_objectives.a: privacy and security notices required by CUI-specified rules are identified, consistent, and associated with the specific CUI category
assessment_objectives.b: privacy and security notices are displayed

examine: Privacy and security policies, procedures addressing system use notification
Documented approval of system use notification messages or banners
User acknowledgements of notification message or banner
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with responsibility for providing legal advice
test: Mechanisms implementing system use notification

requirements.name: Session Lock
requirements.statement: Use session lock with pattern-hiding displays to prevent access and viewing of data after a period of inactivity.

assessment_objectives.a: the period of inactivity after which the system initiates a session lock is defined
assessment_objectives.b: access to the system and viewing of data is prevented by initiating a session lock after the defined period of inactivity
assessment_objectives.c: previously visible information is concealed via a pattern-hiding display after the defined period of inactivity

examine: System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Mechanisms implementing access control policy for session lock

requirements.name: Session Termination
requirements.statement: Terminate (automatically) a user session after a defined condition.

assessment_objectives.a: conditions requiring a user session to terminate are defined
assessment_objectives.b: a user session is automatically terminated after any of the defined conditions occur

examine: System configuration settings and associated documentation
List of conditions or trigger events requiring session disconnect
test: Mechanisms implementing user session termination

requirements.name: Control Remote Access
requirements.statement: Monitor and control remote access sessions.

assessment_objectives.a: remote access sessions are permitted
assessment_objectives.b: the types of permitted remote access are identified
assessment_objectives.c: remote access sessions are controlled
assessment_objectives.d: remote access sessions are monitored

examine: Procedures addressing remote access implementation and usage (including restrictions)
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for managing remote access connections
test: Remote access management capability for the system

requirements.name: Remote Access Confidentiality
requirements.statement: Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions.

assessment_objectives.a: cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions are identified
assessment_objectives.b: cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions are implemented

examine: Procedures addressing remote access to the system
System configuration settings and associated documentation
Cryptographic mechanisms and associated configuration documentation
test: Cryptographic mechanisms protecting remote access sessions

requirements.name: Remote Access Routing
requirements.statement: Route remote access via managed access control points.

assessment_objectives.a: managed access control points are identified and implemented
assessment_objectives.b: remote access is routed through managed network access control points

examine: Procedures addressing remote access to the system
List of all managed network access control points
System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Mechanisms routing all remote accesses through managed network access control points

requirements.name: Privileged Remote Access
requirements.statement: Authorize remote execution of privileged commands and remote access to security-relevant information.

assessment_objectives.a: privileged commands authorized for remote execution are identified
assessment_objectives.b: security-relevant information authorized to be accessed remotely is identified
assessment_objectives.c: the execution of the identified privileged commands via remote access is authorized
assessment_objectives.d: access to the identified security-relevant information via remote access is authorized

examine: Procedures addressing remote access to the system
System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Mechanisms implementing remote access management

requirements.name: Wireless Access Authorization
requirements.statement: Authorize wireless access prior to allowing such connections.

assessment_objectives.a: wireless access points are identified
assessment_objectives.b: wireless access is authorized prior to allowing such connections

examine: Procedures addressing wireless access implementation and usage (including restrictions)
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for managing wireless access connections
test: Wireless access management capability for the system

requirements.name: Wireless Access Protection
requirements.statement: Protect wireless access using authentication and encryption.

assessment_objectives.a: wireless access to the system is protected using authentication
assessment_objectives.b: wireless access to the system is protected using encryption

examine: Procedures addressing wireless implementation and usage (including restrictions)
System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Mechanisms implementing wireless access protections to the system

requirements.name: Mobile Device Connection
requirements.statement: Control connection of mobile devices.

assessment_objectives.a: mobile devices that process, store, or transmit CUI are identified
assessment_objectives.b: mobile device connections are authorized
assessment_objectives.c: mobile device connections are monitored and logged

examine: Authorizations for mobile device connections to organizational systems
Procedures addressing access control for mobile device usage (including restrictions)
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel using mobile devices to access organizational systems
test: Access control capability authorizing mobile device connections to organizational systems

requirements.name: Encrypt CUI on Mobile
requirements.statement: Encrypt CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.

assessment_objectives.a: mobile devices and mobile computing platforms that process, store, or transmit CUI are identified
assessment_objectives.b: encryption is employed to protect CUI on identified mobile devices and mobile computing platforms

examine: Procedures addressing access control for mobile devices
System configuration settings and associated documentation
Encryption mechanisms and associated configuration documentation
interview: Personnel with access control responsibilities for mobile devices
test: Encryption mechanisms protecting confidentiality of information on mobile devices

requirements.name: External Connections
requirements.statement: Verify and control/limit connections to and use of external systems.

assessment_objectives.a: connections to external systems are identified
assessment_objectives.b: the use of external systems is identified
assessment_objectives.c: connections to external systems are verified
assessment_objectives.d: the use of external systems is verified
assessment_objectives.e: connections to external systems are controlled/limited
assessment_objectives.f: the use of external systems is controlled/limited

examine: Procedures addressing the use of external systems
Terms and conditions for external systems
List of applications accessible from external systems
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for defining terms and conditions for use of external systems to access organizational systems
test: Mechanisms implementing terms and conditions on use of external systems

requirements.name: Portable Storage Use
requirements.statement: Limit use of portable storage devices on external systems.

assessment_objectives.a: the use of portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems is identified and documented
assessment_objectives.b: limits on the use of portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems are defined
assessment_objectives.c: the use of portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems is limited as defined

examine: Procedures addressing the use of external systems
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for restricting or prohibiting use of organization-controlled storage devices on external systems
test: Mechanisms implementing restrictions on use of portable storage devices

requirements.name: Control Public Information
requirements.statement: Control CUI posted or processed on publicly accessible systems.

assessment_objectives.a: individuals authorized to post or process information on publicly accessible systems are identified
assessment_objectives.b: procedures to ensure CUI is not posted or processed on publicly accessible systems are identified
assessment_objectives.c: a review process is in place prior to posting of any content to publicly accessible systems
assessment_objectives.d: content on publicly accessible systems is reviewed to ensure that it does not include CUI
assessment_objectives.e: mechanisms are in place to remove and address improper posting of CUI

examine: List of users authorized to post publicly accessible content on organizational systems
Records of publicly accessible information reviews
Records of response to nonpublic information on public websites
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for managing publicly accessible information posted on organizational systems
test: Mechanisms implementing management of publicly accessible content

requirements.name: Role-Based Risk Awareness
requirements.statement: Ensure that managers, systems administrators, and users of organizational systems are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities and of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of those systems.

assessment_objectives.a: security risks associated with organizational activities involving CUI are identified
assessment_objectives.b: policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of the system are identified
assessment_objectives.c: managers, systems administrators, and users of the system are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities
assessment_objectives.d: managers, systems administrators, and users of the system are made aware of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of the system

examine: Procedures addressing security awareness training implementation
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for security awareness training
Personnel composing the general system user community
Personnel with responsibilities for role-based awareness training
test: Mechanisms managing security awareness training
Mechanisms managing role-based security training

requirements.name: Role-Based Training
requirements.statement: Ensure that personnel are trained to carry out their assigned information security-related duties and responsibilities.

assessment_objectives.a: information security-related duties, roles, and responsibilities are defined
assessment_objectives.b: information security-related duties, roles, and responsibilities are assigned to designated personnel
assessment_objectives.c: personnel are adequately trained to carry out their assigned information securityrelated duties, roles, and responsibilities

interview: Personnel with responsibilities for role-based security training
Personnel with assigned system security roles and responsibilities
Personnel with responsibilities for security awareness training
Personnel representing the general system user community
test: Mechanisms managing role-based security training
Mechanisms managing security awareness training

requirements.name: Insider Threat Awareness
requirements.statement: Provide security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat.

assessment_objectives.a: potential indicators associated with insider threats are identified
assessment_objectives.b: security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat is provided to managers and employees

examine: Procedures addressing security awareness training implementation
interview: Personnel that participate in security awareness training
Personnel with responsibilities for basic security awareness training
test: Mechanisms managing insider threat training

requirements.name: System Auditing
requirements.statement: Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity.

assessment_objectives.a: audit logs needed (i.e., event types to be logged) to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity are specified
assessment_objectives.b: the content of audit records needed to support monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity is defined
assessment_objectives.c: audit records are created (generated)
assessment_objectives.d: audit records, once created, contain the defined content
assessment_objectives.e: retention requirements for audit records are defined
assessment_objectives.f: audit records are retained as defined

examine: System configuration settings and associated documentation
Procedures addressing control of audit records
interview: Personnel with audit and accountability responsibilities
Personnel with audit review, analysis and reporting responsibilities
test: Mechanisms implementing system audit logging

requirements.name: User Accountability
requirements.statement: Ensure that the actions of individual system users can be uniquely traced to those users so they can be held accountable for their actions.

assessment_objectives.a: the content of the audit records needed to support the ability to uniquely trace users to their actions is defined
assessment_objectives.b: audit records, once created, contain the defined content

examine: Procedures addressing audit records and event types
System configuration settings and associated documentation
Procedures addressing audit review, analysis, and reporting
interview: Personnel with audit and accountability responsibilities
test: Mechanisms implementing system audit logging

requirements.name: Event Review
requirements.statement: Review and update logged events.

assessment_objectives.a: a process for determining when to review logged events is defined
assessment_objectives.b: event types being logged are reviewed in accordance with the defined review process
assessment_objectives.c: event types being logged are updated based on the review

examine: Procedures addressing audit records and event types
List of organization-defined event types to be logged
Reviewed and updated records of logged event types
interview: Personnel with audit and accountability responsibilities
test: Mechanisms supporting review and update of logged event types

requirements.name: Audit Failure Alerting
requirements.statement: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure.

assessment_objectives.a: personnel or roles to be alerted in the event of an audit logging process failure are identified
assessment_objectives.b: types of audit logging process failures for which alert will be generated are defined
assessment_objectives.c: identified personnel or roles are alerted in the event of an audit logging process failure

examine: Procedures addressing response to audit logging processing failures
System configuration settings and associated documentation
List of personnel to be notified in case of an audit logging processing failure
interview: Personnel with audit and accountability responsibilities
test: Mechanisms implementing system response to audit logging process failures

requirements.name: Audit Correlation
requirements.statement: Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of unlawful, unauthorized, suspicious, or unusual activity.

assessment_objectives.a: audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of unlawful, unauthorized, suspicious, or unusual activity are defined
assessment_objectives.b: defined audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes are correlated

examine: Procedures addressing audit record review, analysis, and reporting
System configuration settings and associated documentation
Procedures addressing investigation of and response to suspicious activities
System audit logs and records across different repositories
interview: Personnel with audit record review, analysis, and reporting responsibilities
test: Mechanisms supporting analysis and correlation of audit records
Mechanisms integrating audit review, analysis and reporting

requirements.name: Reduction & Reporting
requirements.statement: Provide audit record reduction and report generation to support on-demand analysis and reporting.

assessment_objectives.a: an audit record reduction capability that supports on-demand analysis is provided
assessment_objectives.b: a report generation capability that supports on-demand reporting is provided

examine: Procedures addressing audit record reduction and report generation
System configuration settings and associated documentation
Audit record reduction, review, analysis, and reporting tools
interview: Personnel with audit record reduction and report generation responsibilities
test: Audit record reduction and report generation capability

requirements.name: Authoritative Time Source
requirements.statement: Provide a system capability that compares and synchronizes internal system clocks with an authoritative source to generate time stamps for audit records.

assessment_objectives.a: internal system clocks are used to generate time stamps for audit records
assessment_objectives.b: an authoritative source with which to compare and synchronize internal system clocks is specified
assessment_objectives.c: internal system clocks used to generate time stamps for audit records are compared to and synchronized with the specified authoritative time source

examine: System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Mechanisms implementing time stamp generation
Mechanisms implementing internal information system clock synchronization

requirements.name: Audit Protection
requirements.statement: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

assessment_objectives.a: audit information is protected from unauthorized access
assessment_objectives.b: audit information is protected from unauthorized modification
assessment_objectives.c: audit information is protected from unauthorized deletion
assessment_objectives.d: audit logging tools are protected from unauthorized access
assessment_objectives.e: audit logging tools are protected from unauthorized modification
assessment_objectives.f: audit logging tools are protected from unauthorized deletion

examine: Procedures addressing protection of audit information
System configuration settings and associated documentation, system audit logs and records
interview: Personnel with audit and accountability responsibilities
test: Mechanisms implementing audit information protection

requirements.name: Audit Management
requirements.statement: Limit management of audit logging functionality to a subset of privileged users.

assessment_objectives.a: a subset of privileged users granted access to manage audit logging functionality is defined
assessment_objectives.b: management of audit logging functionality is limited to the defined subset of privileged users

examine: Procedures addressing protection of audit information
System configuration settings and associated documentation
System-generated list of privileged users with access to management of audit logging functionality
interview: Personnel with audit and accountability responsibilities
test: Mechanisms managing access to audit logging functionality

requirements.name: System Baselining
requirements.statement: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems (including hardware, software, firmware, and documentation) throughout the respective system development life cycles.

assessment_objectives.a: a baseline configuration is established
assessment_objectives.b: the baseline configuration includes hardware, software, firmware, and documentation
assessment_objectives.c: the baseline configuration is maintained (reviewed and updated) throughout the system development life cycle
assessment_objectives.d: a system inventory is established
assessment_objectives.e: the system inventory includes hardware, software, firmware, and documentation
assessment_objectives.f: the inventory is maintained (reviewed and updated) throughout the system development life cycle

examine: Procedures addressing the baseline configuration of the system
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for establishing the system inventory
Personnel with responsibilities for updating the system inventory
test: Organizational processes for managing baseline configurations
Mechanisms supporting configuration control of the baseline configuration
Organizational processes for developing and documenting an inventory of system components
Organizational processes for updating inventory of system components
Mechanisms supporting or implementing the system inventory
Mechanisms implementing updating of the system inventory

requirements.name: Security Configuration Enforcement
requirements.statement: Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems.

assessment_objectives.a: security configuration settings for information technology products employed in the system are established and included in the baseline configuration
assessment_objectives.b: security configuration settings for information technology products employed in the system are enforced

examine: Procedures addressing configuration settings for the system
System configuration settings and associated documentation
Evidence supporting approved deviations from established configuration settings
interview: Personnel with security configuration management responsibilities
test: Organizational processes for managing configuration settings
Mechanisms that implement, monitor, and/or control system configuration settings
Mechanisms that identify and/or document deviations from established configuration settings
Processes for managing baseline configurations
Mechanisms supporting configuration control of baseline configurations

requirements.name: System Change Management
requirements.statement: Track, review, approve or disapprove, and log changes to organizational systems.

assessment_objectives.a: changes to the system are tracked
assessment_objectives.b: changes to the system are reviewed
assessment_objectives.c: changes to the system are approved or disapproved
assessment_objectives.d: changes to the system are logged

examine: Procedures addressing system configuration change control
Change control audit and review reports
Agenda/minutes from configuration change control oversight meetings
interview: Personnel with configuration change control responsibilities
Members of change control board or similar
test: Organizational processes for configuration change control
Mechanisms that implement configuration change control

requirements.name: Security Impact Analysis
requirements.statement: Analyze the security impact of changes prior to implementation.

assessment_objectives.a: the security impact of changes to the system is analyzed prior to implementation

examine: Procedures addressing security impact analysis for system changes
interview: Personnel with responsibility for conducting security impact analysis
test: Organizational processes for security impact analysis

requirements.name: Access Restrictions for Change
requirements.statement: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to organizational systems.

assessment_objectives.a: physical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are defined
assessment_objectives.b: physical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are documented
assessment_objectives.c: physical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are approved
assessment_objectives.d: physical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are enforced
assessment_objectives.e: logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are defined
assessment_objectives.f: logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are documented
assessment_objectives.g: logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are approved
assessment_objectives.h: logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are enforced

examine: Procedures addressing access restrictions for changes to the system
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with logical access control responsibilities
Personnel with physical access control responsibilities
test: Organizational processes for managing access restrictions associated with changes to the system
Mechanisms supporting, implementing, and enforcing access restrictions associated with changes to the system

requirements.name: Least Functionality
requirements.statement: Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring organizational systems to provide only essential capabilities.

assessment_objectives.a: essential system capabilities are defined based on the principle of least functionality
assessment_objectives.b: the system is configured to provide only the defined essential capabilities

examine: Procedures addressing least functionality in the system
System configuration settings and associated documentation
interview: Personnel with security configuration management responsibilities
test: Organizational processes prohibiting or restricting functions, ports, protocols, or services
Mechanisms implementing restrictions or prohibition of functions, ports, protocols, or services

requirements.name: Nonessential Functionality
requirements.statement: Restrict, disable, or prevent the use of nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services.

assessment_objectives.a: essential programs are defined
assessment_objectives.b: the use of nonessential programs is defined
assessment_objectives.c: the use of nonessential programs is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined
assessment_objectives.d: essential functions are defined
assessment_objectives.e: the use of nonessential functions is defined
assessment_objectives.f: the use of nonessential functions is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined
assessment_objectives.h: the use of nonessential ports is defined
assessment_objectives.i: the use of nonessential ports is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined
assessment_objectives.k: the use of nonessential protocols is defined
assessment_objectives.l: the use of nonessential protocols is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined
assessment_objectives.n: the use of nonessential services is defined
assessment_objectives.o: the use of nonessential services is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined

examine: Procedures addressing least functionality in the system
System configuration settings and associated documentation
Specifications for preventing software program execution
Documented reviews of programs, functions, ports, protocols, and/or services
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for reviewing programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services on the system
test: Organizational processes for reviewing and disabling nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, or services
Mechanisms implementing review and handling of nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, or services
Organizational processes preventing program execution on the system
Organizational processes for software program usage and restrictions
Mechanisms supporting or implementing software program usage and restrictions
Mechanisms preventing program execution on the system

requirements.name: Application Execution Policy
requirements.statement: Apply deny-by-exception (blacklisting) policy to prevent the use of unauthorized software or deny-all, permit-by-exception (whitelisting) policy to allow the execution of authorized software.

assessment_objectives.a: a policy specifying whether whitelisting or blacklisting is to be implemented is specified
assessment_objectives.b: the software allowed to execute under whitelisting or denied use under blacklisting is specified
assessment_objectives.c: whitelisting to allow the execution of authorized software or blacklisting to prevent the use of unauthorized software is implemented as specified

examine: Procedures addressing least functionality in the system
System configuration settings and associated documentation
List of software programs not authorized to execute on the system
List of software programs authorized to execute on the system
Review and update records associated with list of authorized or unauthorized software programs
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for identifying software authorized or not authorized to execute on the system
test: Organizational process for identifying, reviewing, and updating programs authorized or not authorized to execute on the system
Process for implementing blacklisting or whitelisting
Mechanisms supporting or implementing blacklisting or whitelisting

requirements.name: User-Installed Software
requirements.statement: Control and monitor user-installed software.

assessment_objectives.a: a policy for controlling the installation of software by users is established
assessment_objectives.b: installation of software by users is controlled based on the established policy
assessment_objectives.c: installation of software by users is monitored

examine: System configuration settings and associated documentation
List of rules governing user-installed software
interview: Personnel with responsibilities for governing user-installed software
Personnel operating, using, or maintaining the system
Personnel monitoring compliance with user-installed software policy
test: Organizational processes governing user-installed software on the system
Mechanisms enforcing rules or methods for governing the installation of software by users
Mechanisms monitoring policy compliance

requirements.name: Identification
requirements.statement: Identify system users, processes acting on behalf of users, and devices.

assessment_objectives.a: system users are identified
assessment_objectives.b: processes acting on behalf of users are identified
assessment_objectives.c: devices accessing the system are identified

examine: Procedures addressing user identification and authentication
System security plan, system design documentation
System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Organizational processes for uniquely identifying and authenticating users
Mechanisms supporting or implementing identification and authentication capability

requirements.name: Authentication
requirements.statement: Authenticate (or verify) the identities of users, processes, or devices, as a prerequisite to allowing access to organizational systems.

assessment_objectives.a: the identity of each user is authenticated or verified as a prerequisite to system access
assessment_objectives.b: the identity of each process acting on behalf of a user is authenticated or verified as a prerequisite to system access
assessment_objectives.c: the identity of each device accessing or connecting to the system is authenticated or verified as a prerequisite to system access

examine: Procedures addressing user identification and authentication
System configuration settings and associated documentation
Change control records associated with managing system authenticators
test: Mechanisms supporting or implementing authenticator management capability

requirements.name: Multifactor Authentication
requirements.statement: Use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged accounts and for network access to non-privileged accounts.

assessment_objectives.a: privileged accounts are identified
assessment_objectives.b: multifactor authentication is implemented for local access to privileged accounts
assessment_objectives.c: multifactor authentication is implemented for network access to privileged accounts
assessment_objectives.d: multifactor authentication is implemented for network access to non-privileged accounts

examine: Procedures addressing user identification and authentication
System configuration settings and associated documentation
test: Mechanisms supporting or implementing authenticator management capability

requirements.name: Replay-Resistant Authentication
